title: Create OS module guided tour — 6 steps from welcome to first 'aha moment'
description: The OnboardingOverlay (299 lines) exists but only covers System Design. OS module has ZERO onboarding steps. A complete beginner opens OS and sees 'Arr: 0, Burst: 5' with no explanation. Create a 6-step OS-specific tour: welcome → sidebar concepts → process cards explained → Run button → Gantt chart explanation → Compare All prompt. Use existing onboarding-overlay.tsx infrastructure with data-onboarding attributes on OS elements.
acceptanceCriteria: 6-step tour specific to OS module triggers on first visit (useFirstEncounter)
Step 1: Welcome — 'Explore how your computer manages processes, memory, and resources'
Step 2: Sidebar spotlight — '6 OS topics to explore, starting with CPU Scheduling'
Step 3: Process cards spotlight — 'These are processes with arrival times and burst times'
Step 4: Run button spotlight — 'Press Run to see FCFS schedule these processes'
Step 5: Auto-runs FCFS, spotlights Gantt — 'Each block is a process using the CPU'
Step 6: Compare All spotlight — 'Try Compare All to see which algorithm is best'
Can skip at any step. Can replay from settings/help menu.
Works on mobile viewport (375px)

title: Rewrite all 8 empty states with actionable CTAs and concept-specific illustrations
description: All 8 empty states (6 concept canvases + properties + event log) show generic text like 'Run simulation to see metrics.' with a CPU icon. Rewrite each with: concept-specific description, CTA button that runs the default scenario, and micro-illustration. CPU Scheduling: 'How does the OS decide which program runs next? [Run FCFS →]'. Deadlock: 'Two processes hold resources the other needs. [Detect Deadlock →]'.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 6 concept canvases has unique empty state text explaining the concept
Each has a CTA button that runs the default simulation with one click
Properties empty state shows concept-appropriate illustration
Event log empty state explains what events will appear
CTA buttons are prominent (primary color, large enough to notice)
Empty state creates curiosity: 'Discover Belady's Anomaly — where MORE memory causes MORE faults!'

title: Add contextual tooltips to all OS module controls using existing ContextualTooltip system
description: OS module has 4 title= attributes in 3933 lines. No tooltips on algorithm selector, frame count input, reference string input, thread count, permits, or any other control. The ContextualTooltip system (234 lines, first-encounter detection) exists but has zero OS features configured. Add tooltips: 'Arrival Time: when the process enters the ready queue', 'Burst Time: how many time units the process needs', 'Quantum: how long each process runs before being preempted'.
acceptanceCriteria: Algorithm selector: tooltip explaining what each algorithm does
Process card fields: tooltips for Arrival, Burst, Priority
Frame count: 'Number of physical memory frames available'
Reference string: 'Sequence of page numbers the process will access'
Quantum (when RR is selected): 'Time units each process gets before preemption'
Tooltips use useFirstEncounter — show on first visit, can be dismissed
At least 15 tooltips across all 6 concepts

title: Add 'stuck' detection — prompt help after 30 seconds of inactivity
description: If a user stares at the OS module for 30 seconds without clicking anything, they're probably confused. Add a gentle prompt: a subtle slide-in message at the bottom of the canvas saying 'Not sure where to start? [Watch a quick demo →] [Take the tour →]'. Use idle timer with mousemove/click reset. Dismiss on any interaction. Only show once per session.
acceptanceCriteria: After 30s of no clicks/keypresses in OS module, gentle prompt appears
Prompt: 'Not sure where to start?' with [Quick Demo] and [Take Tour] buttons
Quick Demo: auto-runs FCFS with 4 default processes, auto-steps through
Take Tour: triggers the guided tour (OSC-211)
Prompt dismisses on any user interaction
Only shows once per session (localStorage flag)
Does NOT show if user has already run a simulation

title: Add 'Quick Demo' one-click auto-run for each OS concept
description: A beginner should be able to see something impressive with ONE click, zero configuration. Add a 'Watch Demo' button to each concept's empty state that: (1) loads a pre-configured interesting scenario, (2) auto-runs the simulation, (3) auto-steps through at a readable pace with step descriptions highlighted. For CPU Scheduling: runs Compare All on 4 diverse processes. For Page Replacement: runs Belady's Anomaly. For Deadlock: shows a 3-process circular deadlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept has a 'Watch Demo' button in its empty state
CPU Scheduling demo: Compare All on [P1(0,8), P2(1,2), P3(2,1), P4(3,4)]
Page Replacement demo: Belady's Anomaly with the classic ref string
Deadlock demo: 3-process circular deadlock with RAG visualization
Memory demo: 6 address translations showing TLB hit/miss/fault
Mem Alloc demo: 8 requests showing fragmentation buildup
Thread Sync demo: 4 threads competing for mutex with auto-play

title: Add data-onboarding attributes to OS module UI elements for tour targeting
description: The onboarding overlay uses data-onboarding attributes to spotlight UI elements. OS module has ZERO such attributes. Add: data-onboarding='os-sidebar' to OSSidebar, data-onboarding='os-controls' to control bar, data-onboarding='os-run' to Run button, data-onboarding='os-canvas' to canvas area, data-onboarding='os-properties' to properties panel.
acceptanceCriteria: data-onboarding='os-sidebar' on sidebar container
data-onboarding='os-algo-selector' on algorithm dropdown
data-onboarding='os-process-cards' on process input area
data-onboarding='os-run' on Run button
data-onboarding='os-compare' on Compare All button
data-onboarding='os-canvas' on canvas/result area
data-onboarding='os-properties' on properties panel
Spotlight masking works for each target
